title: [CI:DOCS] Update gitleaks baseline w/ harmless findings
body:
This should prevent false-positives on any PRs which reference these commits.

For example: https://github.com/containers/podman/actions/runs/7190206757

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:

```release-note

```

comments.body: Will-it-work (i.e. test changes using gitleaks container) check passed: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6007663574646784?logs=prebuild#L103
@cevich how is this file generated? How will it be updated, maintained, documented?

> @cevich how is this file generated? How will it be updated, maintained, documented?

It's documented in `.gitleaks/README.md`, though perhaps not very clear(?)

The process is really simple: Review the github-action workflow run in question (i.e. from notification mail) and ensure there's nothing "bad" ([example](https://github.com/containers/podman/actions/runs/7190206757)).  Then you download the "report" artifact attached to the workflow run.  Add it's JSON into the baseline JSON.  All or in part.

Note: The items are commit-specific, so if there's a chance a commit ID could change, don't put that into the baseline.  In other words, it's best to stick to commits already on main.
@edsantiago @baude @mheon PTAL.  A few more alerts fired over the holidays that I believe this update would have prevented.
